question.text: Scenario: Arun Kumar is analyzing Reliance Industries (RIL) stock using multiple technical indicators. Current observations: (1) RIL CMP: Rs.2,850. (2) 50-day SMA: Rs.2,780 (rising). 200-day SMA: Rs.2,650 (rising). (3) The 50-day SMA crossed above the 200-day SMA one month ago. (4) RSI: 58. (5) Bollinger Bands: Upper band Rs.2,950, Middle band Rs.2,780, Lower band Rs.2,610. Price is between middle and upper band. (6) Strong support at Rs.2,600 (tested 3 times in last 6 months). (7) Resistance at Rs.2,950 (tested twice). (8) Volume has been above average on recent up-days. (9) MACD is above the signal line and above the zero baseline. Question 1 of 5: The 50-day SMA crossing above the 200-day SMA one month ago is called:

answer.text: Golden cross — a bullish signal confirming the uptrend

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The 50-day MA crossing above the 200-day MA is the Golden Cross — a well-known bullish technical signal. **The Trap:** Golden = bullish (50 above 200). Death = bearish (50 below 200). **Cross-Exam Context:** Ch 15 NISM XV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The golden cross occurred at Rs.2,650-2,700 range. RIL has since moved to Rs.2,850 — the signal has been profitable so far.
